name: ETH Session Drive Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: opening-range or session-window continuation after directional control persists
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing whether one session's directional control can carry into the next leg even in a chop-dominant market, provided price holds above fast value after the initial drive. The edge is not blind continuation; it is continuation only after directional control survives the handoff.
invalidation_condition: Abort if session-drive continuation is too sparse, if post-drive holds fail immediately, or if follow-through is too weak after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional expansion phase establishes control, price holds above fast value rather than fully mean-reverting, and a second continuation leg follows.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in broad chop, some directional windows persist for one more leg after the initial drive. Filtering for hold-after-drive should capture those without depending on full trend regimes.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is appropriate because directional handoff structure is visible and cleaner than on thinner alts while still tradable in a choppy environment.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market mean-reverts immediately after each drive, the continuation thesis will fail because the hold never earns a second leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are valid but second-leg travel is weak, tighten exits and shorten hold time before changing the continuation grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest set of continuation entries with front-half harvesting doing most of the work and only occasional runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h and one confirmation lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after the drive-hold sequence.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated hold entries that fail to extend.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; drive, hold, and persistence are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad hold-after-drive logic rather than rare session-specific timestamp choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Best clean lane for directional handoff continuation in the active basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Drive-hold setups show enough density and positive post-cost follow-through.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Setups are too sparse or continuation fails immediately after the hold.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A modest number of directional handoffs that carry into one more leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism generalizes across majors.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows comparable drive-hold continuation behavior.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly cleaner but smaller continuation legs than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same session-drive idea survives on a faster adjacent lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: 1h preserves the mechanism without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: 1h becomes too noisy or too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More signals than 4h with smaller continuation legs.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.14 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.14 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
